output.raw_response:
## Step-by-Step Reasoning

### Step 1: Identify Key Legal Concepts
The question asks about:
- License grants to a **licensee** (or sublicensor)
- Whether those licenses **extend to affiliates** of the licensee/sublicensor
- Affiliate use rights
- Sublicensing rights to affiliates

### Step 2: Domain-Specific Indicators to Search For
- "licensee's affiliates" / "affiliates of licensee"
- "extend to affiliates"
- "affiliate use"
- "licensee and its affiliates"
- "subsidiary use"
- Sublicensing provisions that include affiliates

### Step 3: Scan the Context

Key sections to examine:
- **Section 5.1** (Cross-License of Shared Know-How) — grants licenses with sublicensing rights
- **Section 5.6** (Sublicensing; Assignability) — directly addresses sublicensing to affiliates
- **Section 4.2** (Cross-License of Shared Library Materials) — grants licenses with sublicensing rights
- **Section 5.4** (Cross-Licenses of Software)
- **Section 10.3** (Binding Effect; Third-Party Beneficiaries)

### Step 4: Evaluate Each Match

**Section 5.6(b)** — directly addresses RemainCo sublicensing to its Affiliates (RemainCo as licensee of SpinCo IP):
> "RemainCo may sublicense the Licensed SpinCo Intellectual Property to Affiliates of RemainCo, even if they become Affiliates after the Distribution Date, solely within the scope of its licenses in Article 5, provided that such sublicense shall only be effective for such time as such entity remains an Affiliate of RemainCo..."

**Section 5.6(c)** — directly addresses SpinCo sublicensing to its Affiliates (SpinCo as licensee of RemainCo IP):
> "SpinCo may sublicense the Licensed RemainCo Intellectual Property to Affiliates of SpinCo, even if they become Affiliates after the Distribution Date, solely within the scope of its licenses in Article 5, provided that such sublicense shall only be effective for such time as such entity remains an Affiliate of SpinCo..."

**Section 5.6(a)** — addresses assignability to affiliates:
> "The foregoing licenses shall be assignable in whole or in part only (i) to any Affiliate or (ii) to the extent the licensee transfers to a Third Party all or substantially all of the assets of the business to which such Licensed SpinCo Intellectual Property or the Licensed RemainCo Intellectual Property, as applicable, relates."

**Section 5.1(a)** — grants license with sublicensing rights (foundational grant):
> "SpinCo, for itself and as representative of all other members of the SpinCo Group, hereby grants to RemainCo (x) a perpetual, irrevocable, exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide right and license with the right to grant sublicenses (solely as set forth in Section 5.6)..."

**Section 5.1(b)** — grants license with sublicensing rights (foundational grant):
> "RemainCo, for itself and as representative of all other members of the RemainCo Group, hereby grants to SpinCo (x) a perpetual, irrevocable, exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide right and license with the right to grant sublicenses (solely as set forth in Section 5.6)..."

**Section 4.2** — grants cross-licenses with sublicensing rights:
> "RemainCo, for itself and as representative of all other members of the RemainCo Group, hereby grants to SpinCo (x) a perpetual (subject to Section 4.4), irrevocable, exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide right and license with the right to grant sublicenses (solely as set forth in Section 5.6)..."

**Section 10.3** — affiliates as third-party beneficiaries:
> "Except as expressly set forth in Section 5.10 and except for the Affiliates of the Parties, which are intended to be third party beneficiaries hereunder, this Agreement does not and is not intended to confer any rights or remedies upon any Person other than the Parties."

---

Final Answer:

The foregoing licenses shall be assignable in whole or in part only (i) to any Affiliate or (ii) to the extent the licensee transfers to a Third Party all or substantially all of the assets of the business to which such Licensed SpinCo Intellectual Property or the Licensed RemainCo Intellectual Property, as applicable, relates.

RemainCo may sublicense the Licensed SpinCo Intellectual Property to Affiliates of RemainCo, even if they become Affiliates after the Distribution Date, solely within the scope of its licenses in Article 5, provided that such sublicense shall only be effective for such time as such entity remains an Affiliate of RemainCo, subject to Section 5.6(a)(ii). RemainCo may, and may permit its sublicensees to, sublicense erection and arrangement drawings; form, fit, and function drawings; and product and installation/erection specifications based upon the Licensed SpinCo Intellectual Property to: (i) customers to enable them to use, operate, maintain and repair the equipment, services or other deliverables which incorporate or are derived from the Licensed SpinCo Intellectual Property and which were sold to them by RemainCo or its sublicensees; and (ii) to contractors, subcontractors and vendors to enable them to manufacture, erect, install, service, repair and maintain those products to which the licenses set forth in Article 5 relate.

SpinCo may sublicense the Licensed RemainCo Intellectual Property to Affiliates of SpinCo, even if they become Affiliates after the Distribution Date, solely within the scope of its licenses in Article 5, provided that such sublicense shall only be effective for such time as such entity remains an Affiliate of SpinCo, subject to Section 5.6(a)(ii). SpinCo may, and may permit its sublicensees to, sublicense erection and arrangement drawings; form, fit, and function drawings; and product and installation/erection specifications based upon the Licensed RemainCo Intellectual Property to: (i) customers to enable them to use, operate, maintain and repair the equipment, services or other deliverables which incorporate or are derived from the Licensed RemainCo Intellectual Property and which were sold to them by SpinCo or its sublicensees; and (ii) to contractors, subcontractors and others to enable them to manufacture, erect, install, service, repair and maintain those products to which the license set forth in Article 5 relate.

SpinCo, for itself and as representative of all other members of the SpinCo Group, hereby grants to RemainCo (x) a perpetual, irrevocable, exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide right and license with the right to grant sublicenses (solely as set forth in Section 5.6) to use the SpinCo Know-How currently or previously used in connection with the RemainCo Business or otherwise in the possession of RemainCo or any member of the RemainCo Group as of Distribution Date (the "Licensed SpinCo Know-How"), for the continued operation of the RemainCo Business and any future extensions of the RemainCo Business in the RemainCo Core Field and (y) a perpetual, irrevocable, non-exclusive, royalty-free, worldwide right and license with the right to grant sublicenses (solely as set forth in Section 5.6) to use the Licensed SpinCo Know-How for the continued operation of the RemainCo Business and any future extensions of the RemainCo Business in any field other than the RemainCo Core Field or the SpinCo Core Field.

input.question: Highlight the parts (if any) of this contract related to "Notice Period To Terminate Renewal" that should be reviewed by a lawyer. Details: What is the notice period required to terminate renewal?

output.raw_response:
## Step-by-Step Reasoning:

**Step 1: Identify key legal concepts in the Question**
The question asks about the "notice period required to terminate renewal" — specifically looking for provisions about advance notice needed to prevent automatic renewal or to terminate the agreement upon renewal.

**Step 2: Use domain-specific indicators**
Looking for: notice period, days' notice, written notice, prior to expiration, notice of non-renewal, notice to terminate, advance notice, automatic renewal, opt-out provisions.

**Step 3: Scan the Context for relevant sentences**

- **Section 3 (Term):** States the agreement expires on March 14, 2020, but contains no automatic renewal clause and no notice period for termination of renewal.
- **Section 13 (Insurance):** Mentions "30 days' notice for cancellation of policy and 10 days' notice for non-payment of premium" — but this relates to insurance policy cancellation, not contract renewal termination.
- **Section 14 (Termination):** Discusses termination before expiration with notice periods (10 business days), but does not address renewal termination specifically.
- **No automatic renewal clause** exists in this agreement.

**Step 4: Evaluate relevance**
The contract does **not** contain an automatic renewal provision or a specific notice period to terminate renewal. The agreement simply expires on March 14, 2020. There is no clause requiring advance notice to prevent renewal.

**Step 5: Extract the most directly relevant clause**
The Term clause is the most directly relevant, as it defines the contract's duration and expiration without any renewal mechanism.

---

Final Answer:
This Agreement shall commence on March 15, 2018 and will expire on March 14, 2020 unless terminated earlier pursuant to Section 13 of the Agreement (the "Term").

input.question: Highlight the parts (if any) of this contract related to "Volume Restriction" that should be reviewed by a lawyer. Details: Is there a fee increase or consent requirement, etc. if one party’s use of the product/services exceeds certain threshold?

output.raw_response:
## Step-by-Step Reasoning

**Step 1: Identify key legal concepts in the Question**
The question asks about "Volume Restriction" — specifically whether there are any provisions related to:
- Fee increases if usage exceeds a threshold
- Consent requirements for exceeding certain volume limits
- Caps on quantity/units of services used
- Minimum or maximum order requirements

**Step 2: Apply domain-specific indicators**
Searching for: volume, quantity, minimum order, maximum quantity, units, volume cap, volume limitations, quantity restrictions, threshold, fee adjustment

**Step 3: Scan the Context**
Reviewing all articles for volume-related language:
- Article 4.1 discusses Service Fee calculation and adjustment rights
- No explicit "volume cap" or "quantity restriction" language found
- Article 4.1 contains a fee adjustment clause that could be relevant — Party B can adjust fees "at its own discretion"
- No threshold-based fee increases found
- No consent requirements tied to usage volume found

**Step 4: Evaluate potential matches**
The closest relevant provision is in Article 4.1, where Party B has unilateral discretion to adjust Service Fees without Party A's consent. While not a traditional "volume restriction," this fee adjustment clause is directly relevant to whether fees can increase based on usage, and a lawyer should review it.

**Step 5: Extract exact text**

Final Answer:
Party B shall have the right to charge, on a quarterly basis, the service fee ("Service Fee") from Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services, or designate another person to charge Service Fee from Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services. The total sum of Service Fee shall be equal to the amount of profits gained by Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services. Party B shall have the right to adjust the amount of Service Fee at its own discretion, without the prior consent of Party A or its subsidiaries.

The total sum of Service Fee shall be equal to the amount of profits gained by Party A or its subsidiaries who have accepted Party B's Services. Party B shall have the right to adjust the amount of Service Fee at its own discretion, without the prior consent of Party A or its subsidiaries.

input.question: Highlight the parts (if any) of this contract related to "Price Restrictions" that should be reviewed by a lawyer. Details: Is there a restriction on the  ability of a party to raise or reduce prices of technology, goods, or  services provided?

output.raw_response:
## Step-by-Step Reasoning

### Step 1: Identify Key Legal Concepts
The question asks about **price restrictions** — specifically, limitations on a party's ability to raise or reduce prices of goods/services provided under the contract.

### Step 2: Domain-Specific Indicators to Search For
- Price adjustment mechanisms
- Price change notice requirements
- Price floors or ceilings
- Conditions under which prices can be changed
- Restrictions on when/how prices can be modified

### Step 3: Scan the Context for Relevant Sentences

I need to find clauses that:
- Restrict or govern how prices can be raised or lowered
- Set conditions or limits on price adjustments
- Require notice before price changes
- Cap the amount of price changes

### Step 4: Evaluate Matches

**From the PRICING section (main agreement):**
- The Crude Trigger Clause sets limits on how much prices can increase/decrease based on crude oil price movements — this directly restricts price adjustment ability.
- The base prices are fixed for specific calendar year periods.
- The parties must meet to assess price adjustments for certain years.
- Any permitted adjustments are governed by specific terms.

**From Attachment A, Section 1:**
- Seller may change price with 30 days' notice — this is a restriction on how prices can be changed.

**From Attachment G (PRICE ADJUSTMENT clause):**
- Buyer's failure to object within 10 days constitutes acceptance of price changes.

### Step 5: Extract Exact Relevant Text

Final Answer:

"For calendar years 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022 and 2023, the price of Products sold by Seller/ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates to Buyer/Buyer Affiliates will comprise the Base Price, the crude adjustment and the freight cost depending on Incoterms."

"Product price(s) shall be subject to the Average Brent crude oil price evolution (as further detailed below) in order to reflect the cost of energy. Should the Average Brent crude oil price at any moment during the term of this Agreement move to a different Average Brent crude oil price bracket as mentioned below, Seller may increase or decrease the Product price by $[*****] for every $[*****] change in the Average Brent crude oil price. In no event shall the Product price's increase or decrease exceed $[*****] for every $[*****] change in the Average Brent crude oil price brackets."

"Seller and Buyer shall meet on or before December 31, [*****] to assess the requirements for a price and/or volume adjustment in good faith on the price for Products sold by Seller/ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates to Buyer/Buyer Affiliates for years [*****] and [*****]."

"Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in Attachments A, G and H to this Agreement, the parties agree that any permitted adjustments to the price, freight or payment terms for Products sold hereunder will be governed by the terms of the Pricing and Payment Terms sections of this Agreement."

"Seller may change any price, freight or payment term hereof upon no less than thirty (30) days' prior written notice; provided, however, that Seller may at any time institute or remove a temporary voluntary allowance of other similar competitive allowance without prior notice."

"Buyer's failure to deliver to Seller written objection to any such change at least ten (10) days before its effective date shall constitute acceptance. If Buyer does deliver such objections within the deadline, no delivery shall be made until parties agree on the new price, freight and/or payment terms."

"Products shall be supplied by EM at the price valid on the planned delivery date."

input.question: Highlight the parts (if any) of this contract related to "Affiliate License-Licensee" that should be reviewed by a lawyer. Details: Does the contract contain a license grant to a licensee (incl. sublicensor) and the affiliates of such licensee/sublicensor?

output.raw_response:
## Step-by-Step Reasoning:

**Step 1: Identify key legal concepts in the Question**
The question asks about:
- License grants to a licensee
- Whether the license extends to affiliates of the licensee/sublicensor
- Sublicensing rights

**Step 2: Use domain-specific indicators**
Looking for:
- "licensee's affiliates"
- "affiliates of licensee"
- "extend to affiliates"
- "affiliate use"
- "licensee and its affiliates"
- "subsidiary use"
- License grant language

**Step 3: Scan the Context for relevant sentences**

The contract contains license grant language in Section 13. Let me examine it:

- Section 13.1: Appoints Customer as "sole and exclusive agent for the promotion, sales, marketing, distribution and administration"
- Section 13.2: Grants "exclusive rights to the Customer for the term of ten (10) years"

**Step 4: Evaluate relevance**

The contract contains a license/exclusive rights grant to the Customer (licensee), but there is **no mention** of affiliates of the Customer/licensee, no sublicensing rights, and no extension of rights to subsidiaries or affiliates.

**Step 5: Extract relevant sentences**

The license grant clauses are directly relevant to the question, even though affiliate language is absent — a lawyer should review these clauses precisely because affiliate/sublicensing rights are not addressed.

Final Answer:
The Manufacturer hereby appoints the Customer to be the sole and exclusive agent for the promotion, sales, marketing, distribution and administration of the products listed in schedule A of this agreement based on minimum annual product purchase requirements as listed in Schedule B of this agreement.
The Manufacturer grants exclusive rights to the Customer for the term of ten (10) years from the date of the signing of this agreement and for an indefinite period upon the customer fulfilling the minimum annual purchase requirement as listed in Schedule B. of this agreement.
